tool.name: infoassembly
tool.summary: Normalize provider-backed archive metadata into a bounded assembly-first report

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `infoassembly` is the assembly-first archive metadata companion to `runinfo`. In v1 it resolves one provider-qualified archive accession through the governed archive-provider seam and projects normalized archive metadata into a bounded assembly-first report.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts exactly one provider-qualified archive accession such as `ena:ERR123456` or `sra:SRR123456`. Local file inputs and inline literals are rejected deliberately because `infoassembly` is a provider-backed metadata lookup tool.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a stable `field`/`value` table report plus provider-summary lines. The bounded v1 surface reports the selected assembly identifier, linked archive identifiers, library metadata when present, file counts, total known bytes, and the normalized provider route label.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This bounded release keeps one historical `infoassembly` user need in scope while modernizing around deterministic normalized assembly-first metadata instead of broad archive-schema or acquisition behavior. The committed compared example stays narrow and provider-aware: one mocked ENA accession is resolved through the governed archive seam and compared against a checked-in expected table payload.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema infoassembly`. The bounded mocked ENA assembly-first path is compared against a committed expected table fixture through the acceptance-anchor harness, and Rust service coverage also exercises mocked ENA and mocked SRA metadata paths through the same governed archive-provider seam. That proves the shipped retrieval surface without claiming broad provider parity or hidden live-network validation.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 surface is intentionally conservative. `infoassembly` does not accept local files, free-text metadata blobs, or hidden live-network fallback behavior. It projects assembly-first metadata from the current normalized archive seam and does not yet claim broad provider-parity or full assembly-schema completeness.

artifacts.label: Mocked ENA infoassembly case fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note for the mocked ENA assembly-first metadata path used in Rust service coverage.

examples.title: Normalize ENA archive metadata into a bounded assembly-first report
examples.description: Resolves a provider-qualified ENA archive accession and returns the current governed assembly-first metadata table and provider summary.

expected_outputs.label: Normalized ENA assembly-first metadata table
expected_outputs.description: A stable `field`/`value` report that includes the selected assembly accession, linked archive identifiers, file counts, total known bytes, and the provider route label.
